description:
You are acting as a junior backup-engineer who has just been
        handed a small data-set that lives in

        /home/user/project_data

        The directory already exists and contains exactly three plain-text
        files:

        • /home/user/project_data/data1.txt  
        • /home/user/project_data/data2.txt  
        • /home/user/project_data/config.cfg  

        Your job is to archive the directory and create a verification
        log so that a senior engineer can later confirm the data was
        backed-up correctly.

        REQUIRED END-STATE
        ------------------
        1. A new directory called /home/user/backup must exist.
        2. Inside that directory there must be a single gzip-compressed
           tar archive named

           /home/user/backup/project_data_backup.tar.gz

           It MUST store the directory structure exactly as it appears
           on disk, i.e. when someone lists the archive with

               tar -tzf /home/user/backup/project_data_backup.tar.gz

           they must see (in this order):

               project_data/data1.txt
               project_data/data2.txt
               project_data/config.cfg

        3. A plain-text verification log called

           /home/user/backup/backup_verification.log

           must be produced **in the exact format shown below**.
           The automated tests will parse the file byte-for-byte, so
           every space, newline and label must match.

           --------  REQUIRED log format  --------
           DATE: YYYY-MM-DD
           FILE CHECKSUMS:
           <sha256_of_data1.txt>  project_data/data1.txt
           <sha256_of_data2.txt>  project_data/data2.txt
           <sha256_of_config.cfg>  project_data/config.cfg

           ARCHIVE_CONTENTS:
           project_data/data1.txt
           project_data/data2.txt
           project_data/config.cfg
           --------  END of log format  --------

           Formatting rules:
           • Replace YYYY-MM-DD with today’s date in ISO-8601 form.
           • Each SHA-256 checksum must be calculated from the current
             on-disk contents of the individual file, **not** from the
             archive.
           • There are two spaces between each checksum and the path
             (standard “sha256sum” output).
           • There is a single blank line between the FILE CHECKSUMS
             section and the ARCHIVE_CONTENTS section.
           • No extra blank lines at end of file.

        ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
        -------------------
        • /home/user/backup/project_data_backup.tar.gz exists and can be
          extracted without error.
        • The archive contains exactly the three files listed above and
          nothing else.
        • /home/user/backup/backup_verification.log exists and matches
          the required layout **exactly**.
        • The SHA-256 values recorded in the log file match the actual
          checksums of the three source files.
        • The list under “ARCHIVE_CONTENTS:” matches the output of
          tar -tzf on the archive.

        HINTS (you do not have to follow them, but they are consistent
        with a 3-to-4 command solution):
        • mkdir -p for the destination directory
        • tar -czf to create the archive
        • sha256sum to generate checksums
        • tar -tzf to capture archive contents

        Remember: the automated grader will reject the solution if even
        one character deviates from the specified log layout, or if the
        archive structure / file checksums do not match what is on disk.
truth:
Pre-existing files and their exact contents (including the final
        newline in each file) before the agent starts:

        Path: /home/user/project_data/data1.txt
        Content:
        The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog
        (newline)

        Path: /home/user/project_data/data2.txt
        Content:
        Sphinx of black quartz, judge my vow
        (newline)

        Path: /home/user/project_data/config.cfg
        Content:
        mode=production
        (newline)

        All three files are world-readable (0644) and owned by user:user.

        The test-suite will:
        1. Verify that /home/user/backup exists.
        2. Verify that /home/user/backup/project_data_backup.tar.gz can
           be listed and extracted and contains exactly the three files
           above with paths prefixed by project_data/.
        3. Open /home/user/backup/backup_verification.log and check:
           • The first line matches  ^DATE: \d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}\n$
           • The three checksum lines match
                ^[a-f0-9]{64}  project_data/[^ ]+\.txt\n
             (and cfg respectively)
           • The checksum recorded for each file equals the checksum
             the grader recomputes directly from the on-disk source
             file content shown above.
           • The ARCHIVE_CONTENTS section contains the three required
             lines in the specified order, followed by a final newline
             and no extra data.
name: task_000000_aac2ffcc
